why_them: Family-run nursery with 28+ years of operation, currently on a free Wix subdomain (info82223.wixsite.com/happyfaces) with no custom domain — a significant credibility gap for an established provider. No online enrolment or pricing visible. A nursery of this age and reputation deserves a professional web presence to match.
recommended_service: Professional branded website with custom domain, online enrolment form, and fees page
source: Yell.com, DumfriesPages directory, Wix subdomain confirmed

why_them: Established nursery rated 'Very Good' by SCSWIS, serving children 0-5 years with capacity for 28 — yet has no own website, appearing only in third-party directories. Parents searching online cannot find a direct digital presence, undermining trust and enquiry conversion. Linked to Malory House Nursery, suggesting a multi-site operation with real budget.
recommended_service: New website with about page, online enquiry form, Care Inspectorate rating display, and photo gallery

location: Former Johnston Primary School, St Mary's Street, Kirkcudbright, DG6 4EG
why_them: Private nursery for children birth to 5 years in Kirkcudbright with no website — only a Facebook page. Operates from a former school building suggesting established multi-room capacity. Parents in Kirkcudbright and Stewartry area searching 'nursery near me' will not find them organically without a website.
recommended_service: New website with enrolment enquiry form, gallery of the premises, Care Inspectorate link, and Google Maps integration
source: D&G Council ELC directory, Facebook page

location: 3A Station Yard, Oakwell Road, Castle Douglas, DG7 1LA
why_them: Registered Community Interest Company operating in two settings (Castle Douglas and Auchencairn) with no independent website — only a Facebook page and hotmail contact address. The CIC structure and dual-site presence indicate a growing, funded organisation that would benefit enormously from a professional digital presence to attract both families and funding bodies.
recommended_service: Website with dual-location pages, parent information, funded hours guidance, and a modern contact form replacing the hotmail address
source: Companies House (SC733062), Castle Douglas Development Forum, D&G Council ELC directory

location: Community Centre, Charles Street, Langholm, DG13 0AA
why_them: Long-established charity (incorporated 1996) offering all-day childcare, breakfast, holiday clubs and out-of-school care. Website returns a self-signed certificate error in 2026, meaning it is effectively inaccessible to parents — a critical digital failure for an organisation that serves children up to age 16. Yell listing confirms they are still trading.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild with SSL, enrolment enquiry forms, holiday club calendar, and funded hours information
source: Yell.com, OSCR charity register (SC024553), D&G Council ELC directory

why_them: Purpose-built family-run nursery (founded 2010) with a functioning website, but no online enrolment and no pricing information — parents must phone or email. The site also covers Moffat Academy cluster after-school care. Adding online booking and a fees page would significantly reduce admin burden and improve conversion from website visits.
recommended_service: Online enrolment form integration, pricing/fees page, and SEO optimisation for Lochmaben/Lockerbie searches
source: jaybeeschildcare.co.uk (fetched), D&G Council ELC directory, The Parliamentary Review

business_name: Kids Ahoy Nursery & After School Care
why_them: Two-site Dumfries operation (nursery + 'Land Ahoy' outdoor nursery) covering children birth to 12 years, with after-school and holiday clubs. The website runs on HTTP (no SSL), fails to load reliably (503 errors in testing), and has no reviews on major third-party platforms — all signs of a neglected digital presence for what is clearly a sizeable business.
recommended_service: HTTPS migration, modern redesign with online registration, parent portal integration, and Google review strategy
source: Yell.com, WhichNursery, D&G Council ELC directory, childcarelocations.co.uk

why_them: Registered limited company nursery in Castle Douglas with a domain (treasureislandnursery.co.uk) that no longer resolves — the website is dead. Rated 'Good' by SCSWIS and listed in the D&G Council directory, they are clearly still trading but have completely lost their web presence. Any parent searching online will hit a dead end.
recommended_service: Full website rebuild on a reliable host with SSL, online enquiry form, Care Inspectorate rating badge, and location/parking info
source: Yell.com, Care Inspectorate, D&G Council ELC directory, domain confirmed dead

why_them: Established Dumfries nursery with a domain (firststepsnurserydumfries.co.uk) that has gone offline — DNS no longer resolves. Listed on Thomson Local and the D&G Council directory as active. A nursery for 3 months to school age operating without a functional website is losing parent enquiries to competitors daily.
recommended_service: New website with age-group room information, staff profiles, online enquiry form, and funded hours guidance
source: Thomson Local, D&G Council ELC directory, domain confirmed dead

why_them: DVSA-registered Dumfries driving school with a website that has a broken outbound link pointing to an unrelated pharmacy — a clear sign of poor maintenance and low trust. No online booking and pricing is buried rather than front-and-centre. Competitors like Dave's and Pro-Motion are also weak, meaning a well-designed site would dominate local search.
recommended_service: Website redesign with clear pricing page, online lesson booking/enquiry, and trust signals (pass rates, testimonials)

location: Hutton House, 15 St Marys Place, Kirkcudbright, DG6 4BA
why_them: 20+ year established driving school serving Kirkcudbright, Castle Douglas, Dalbeattie, Dumfries, and Gatehouse of Fleet — a wide coverage area. The website domain failed DNS resolution in testing, meaning it is effectively dead or unreachable. No national chain competitor serves Kirkcudbright so a good local site would dominate organic search.
recommended_service: New website with area coverage map, pricing, online lesson booking enquiry, and instructor bio
source: Thomson Local, Top Driving Schools directory, Yell.com

why_them: Established Dumfries HGV and driver training centre operating since 2001 with 15+ staff and a 90% pass rate. The website is WordPress-built but clearly dated (custom theme named 'nithcree-training-2017'), has no pricing, no online booking, and uses a Gmail address for contact — unprofessional for a company of this scale and reputation.
recommended_service: Website modernisation with course pricing page, online enquiry/booking form, professional branded email, and HGV-specific SEO

location: Unit 3, Catherinefield Industrial Estate, Dumfries, DG1 3PQ
why_them: HGV and plant machinery training business based in Dumfries serving a wide area including Gretna, Lockerbie, and Carlisle. The Elementor-built site has no pricing and no online booking — all enquiries require a phone call. With only two Google reviews despite trading history, the site is not generating organic trust or enquiries.
recommended_service: Course pricing pages, online booking enquiry system, review generation strategy, and Google Business Profile optimisation

location: Millers Cottage, Braehead, Kirkinner, Newton Stewart, DG8 9AH
why_them: Driving school operating in Newton Stewart (DG8) with no website found — only directory listings and an opening hours aggregator entry. Newton Stewart and Wigtownshire are underserved in terms of local driving school digital presence, giving a well-built site a strong chance to rank first for 'driving lessons Newton Stewart' with minimal competition.
recommended_service: New website with clear pricing, lesson types, contact form, and local SEO targeting Newton Stewart and Wigtown area searches

why_them: Registered private nursery in Dumfries with after-school care and holiday clubs, serving children to age 12. The website domain (graciedrewnursery.com) returned a 404 error during testing — the site is offline or broken despite being widely listed in directories. With a Facebook page but no working website, parents researching online hit a dead end.
recommended_service: New website rebuild with services overview, age groups, online enquiry form, Care Inspectorate information, and social media integration
